mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 5.1% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which depress corporate earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.1%, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates weak aggregate demand, creating a negative feedback loop that reduces equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and HOUST fails to explain more than 10% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or 60d returns show positive correlation with the composite signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below 111.9137 signals reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which lowers corporate margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 111.9137, or the signal's IC drops below -0.07 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) compress bond yields, diverting capital from equities and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal's IC remains above -0.05 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) below 137,846 thousand signals weak labor demand, which reduces consumer spending and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS falls below 137846, or the signal's IC drops below -0.06 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL > 212.571) and consumer spending (RSAFS > 381416) indicates overheating demand, which triggers central bank intervention and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and RSAFS fails to explain more than 15% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or 20d returns show positive correlation with the composite signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 111.9137 signals strong manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 60d returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 111.9137, or the signal's IC drops below 0.07 in rolling 3-year windows.
